LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SERGIO AZZOLLINI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: COURT STRIKES DEFENDANT'S AMENDED OPPOSITION AND HICKS REPLY DECLARATION AS UNTIMELY. COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVEN A PRIMA FACIE CASE FOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SERGIO AZZOLLINI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Justin L. Allamano, Esq. of Luce Forward, (415) 356-4600, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Anthony M. Leones, Esq. of Miller Starr Regalia, (925) 935-9400, appearing for Defendant, CBS Outdoor, Inc., defendant and opposing party; Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Court strikes defendant's amended opposition and Hicks reply declaration as untimely. Court Grants plaintiff's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff has proven a prima facie case for unlawful detainer and defendant fails to create a triable issue of fact. Order signed in Open Court. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Feb-23-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
